mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M340i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M340i (B58) engine typically costs £4,000–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,500 depending on location and workshop rates. Dealer estimates for complete B58 engine replacement are commonly reported in the £14,000–£18,000 range. Reconditioned B58 engines from specialists typically cost £5,500–£8,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M340i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. It was named to Ward's 10 Best Engines four times, including in 2020 for the M340i. The B58 has proven itself to be very capable, versatile, and one of the most reliable BMW engines ever built. However, common issues include coolant system leaks from plastic connectors, oil filter housing and valve cover gasket leaks, and VANOS solenoid wear.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW M340i?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Coolant system leaks from plastic connectors behind the heat management module — the most frequent failure point, (2) Oil filter housing leaks and valve cover gasket leaks — plastic components age and gaskets harden, and (3) VANOS solenoid O-ring degradation — causing power loss and rough idle.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW M340i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £25,000–£40,000 M340i, a £5,000–£6,500 recon is 15–25% of the car's value — worth it given the M340i's strong retained values. For higher-mileage examples, evaluate the car's overall condition before committing. The B58's robust reliability means that with proper maintenance, these engines can achieve very high mileages.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW M340i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M340i uses the B58B30O1 engine — a 3.0-litre turbocharged inline-six. Pre-LCI cars (2019–2022) use the B58TU1 variant; LCI cars (2022–present) use the B58TU2 variant with 48V mild-hybrid. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Does the B58 have a cambelt or timing chain?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 uses a timing chain, not a cambelt. The B58TU1 introduced a redesigned one-piece timing chain arrangement. The chain is designed to last the life of the engine and does not require routine replacement. Unlike BMW's earlier turbo engines (N54, N55), the B58's timing chain is not a common failure point. However, VANOS solenoid issues can affect timing performance.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an M340i engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: Coolant System Leaks (Plastic Connector Failure)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning light, coolant level dropping, visible coolant residue under the vehicle or around the engine bay, overheating if ignored
problems.typicalMileage: Can occur from 40,000 miles — heat cycling and hydrolysis weaken plastic components
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (coolant hose connector replacement, thermostat housing, or water pump)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant level drops frequently

problems.recommendation: One of the most frequent failure points on the B58 is the coolant hose connector behind the heat management module. The OE plastic connector is prone to cracking due to hydrolysis and repeated heat cycles. Coolant leaks are common across the B58 platform, with some owners reporting sudden coolant loss from the primary reservoir leading to overheating. An upgraded aluminium connector eliminates this failure point. If you're topping up coolant regularly, have the system pressure-tested and inspect the plastic connectors behind the intake manifold.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide → /failures/b58-coolant-leak/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Oil Filter Housing & Valve Cover Gasket Leaks
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell from the engine bay, visible oil residue around the valve cover or oil filter housing, low oil level warning
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — plastic components age and gaskets harden
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing replacement)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil leaks or burning smell

problems.recommendation: The B58's plastic oil filter housing is a known weakness, with the component appearing to have an average lifespan of around eight years. BMW is facing a class action lawsuit over faulty oil filter housings that leak coolant. Valve cover gasket leaks are also common due to heat cycling and gasket deterioration. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Catch these leaks early to prevent oil contamination of other components.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide → /failures/b58-oil-leaks/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: VANOS Solenoid Failure
problems.symptoms: Power loss during acceleration, rough idle, slow throttle response, check engine light with VANOS-related fault codes
problems.recommendation: The VANOS solenoid's rubber O-ring deteriorates over time due to heat and oil contamination. BMW recommends replacing VANOS seals every 80,000 km as preventive maintenance. Symptoms include noticeable power loss, rough idle, and slow throttle response. If you experience these issues, have the VANOS solenoids inspected and replaced with updated BMW parts. This is a relatively straightforward repair that can restore full engine performance.

failureLink.label: Read the full VANOS failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This M340i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with BMW-approved oil and maintenance schedule
* Evidence of coolant system inspection or upgraded aluminium connectors fitted
* No oil leaks from the valve cover or oil filter housing
* Remaining manufacturer warranty — these are complex performance cars
avoidIf: * Frequent coolant top-ups with no documented fix — plastic connectors are a known weakness
* Visible oil leaks from the front or top of the engine
* Check engine light with VANOS-related fault codes
* Poor service history — the B58 is reliable but needs proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M340i's B58 is one of BMW's finest modern engines — a 3.0-litre turbocharged inline-six with 374 PS that delivers effortless performance and genuine character. The B58 has proven itself to be "very capable, versatile, and one of the most reliable BMW engines ever built". However, it has well-documented weak points: plastic coolant connectors that crack with age, oil filter housing and valve cover gasket leaks, and VANOS solenoid wear. Buy with full service history and evidence of preventative maintenance, and you'll have one of the most rewarding performance saloons on the road.

cta.label: → Compare M340i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW M340i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant Level — Check for frequent top-ups; inspect for leaks around the heat management module
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve cover and oil filter housing
* Cold Start — Listen for any unusual rattling or ticking (B58 timing chain is generally robust)
* Service History — Including the B58 engine and confirmed BMW-approved oil changes
* VANOS Health — Any power loss, rough idle, or hesitation? Check for fault codes
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for VANOS or cooling system codes
* Brake & Transmission — Check for smooth gear changes; ZF 8HP is robust but benefits from fluid changes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
